sm: Largest hyperscaler by cloud-infrastructure-services revenue; ~29% worldwide share in Q3 2025, gradually eroding from ~32% in 2021 as Azure and GCP close the gap.
Second hyperscaler; ~20% worldwide CIS share Q3 2025, gaining share. Bundles compute with M365/Entra identity and Trusted Signing distribution gates.

s: Google ~13% Q3 worldwide CIS share; ~4x Alibaba
sm: Third hyperscaler; ~13% worldwide CIS share Q3 2025, gaining share and ~4x the size of 4th-placed Alibaba. Free intra-multi-region read traffic differentiates egress.

s: OCI 10TB/mo free egress, $0.0085/GB beyond, flat global pricing
sm: Challenger gaining share alongside neoclouds; differentiates on flat global pricing and aggressive egress (10 TB/mo free, then $0.0085/GB ~10x cheaper than AWS), at the cost of the priciest hot-storage tier of the big four.

s: CoreWeave largest neocloud; Crusoe, Nebius, Lambda growing fast
sm: GPU-specialized challengers inching up share as the big three hold 63%; CoreWeave is the largest. Rent frontier GPUs (H200/MI325X-class) for training and inference outside hyperscaler markup.

Metered per-token inference layer over hyperscaler/neocloud compute. April 2026 list rates span $0.10/1M input (GPT-4.1 Nano, Gemini 2.0 Flash, Mistral Small) to $15/$75 in/out (Claude Opus 4.6); mid-tier GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 Pro at $1.25/$10.

s: Local hardware TCO: M4 Ultra, RTX 5090, MI325X
sm: Owned-hardware inference: Apple Mac Studio M4 Ultra (192GB unified), RTX 5090 (32GB VRAM, $1,999 MSRP, ~$3,350 desktop), dual-5090 (~$6,900, 64GB combined), AMD MI325X (256GB HBM3e, $19k-24k). Serving via Ollama (light) or vLLM (production, +20-40% throughput).

s: Big three 63% of enterprise cloud-infra spend Q3 2025
sm: AWS+Azure+GCP together hold ~63% of worldwide cloud-infrastructure spend, so the compute substrate under most inference APIs is concentrated in three owners.

s: Egress often exceeds storage cost for AI/analytics/CDN
sm: Egress fees ($0.09/GB AWS) act as a switching/exit tax that gates data movement off-cloud, structurally favouring keeping data + inference where it already lives.

s: Crossover points where local overtakes cloud
sm: On-device inference competes with metered cloud APIs above a sustained-volume crossover; below it, fixed hardware cost dominates and cloud per-use wins.

stmt: In Q3 2025 Synergy Research put worldwide cloud-infrastructure-services share at AWS 29%, Microsoft 20%, Google 13% (big three combined 63%, up from 61% two years prior). Amazon's share is gradually eroding while Microsoft and Google gain; third-placed Google is ~4x the size of fourth-placed Alibaba.
fx: A later Synergy/Canalys quarter shows the big-three combined share dropping below 60% or AWS regaining >31%.

stmt: Worldwide cloud-infrastructure-services revenue (IaaS+PaaS+hosted private) reached {"cur":"USD","val":1.069e11} in Q3 2025, with trailing-twelve-month revenue of {"cur":"USD","val":3.9e11}; public IaaS+PaaS grew 30% YoY. The US grew 28% and remains larger than all of APAC.
fx: Synergy revises Q3 2025 worldwide CIS materially below 1.0e11 USD or YoY IaaS+PaaS growth below 20%.

s: AWS $0.09/GB egress; OCI 10TB free then $0.0085/GB; hot storage Azure $0.018 GCP $0.020 AWS $0.023 /GB-mo
stmt: Internet egress is priced at {"cur":"USD","val":0.09}/GB on AWS (first 10 TB/mo) versus {"cur":"USD","val":0.0085}/GB on OCI after a 10 TB/mo free allowance (~10x cheaper). Hot object storage runs Azure Blob {"cur":"USD","val":0.018}, GCP {"cur":"USD","val":0.020}, AWS S3 {"cur":"USD","val":0.023} per GB-month; egress frequently exceeds storage cost for AI/analytics/CDN workloads.
fx: AWS cuts standard internet egress below 0.05 USD/GB or removes egress charges for outbound AI inference traffic.

stmt: April 2026 published cloud LLM list rates (USD per 1M input/output tokens): GPT-4.1 Nano 0.10/0.40, Gemini 2.0 Flash 0.10/0.40, Mistral Small 0.10/0.30, GPT-4o Mini 0.15/0.60, Claude Haiku 4.5 0.80/4.00, GPT-5 1.25/10.00, Gemini 2.5 Pro 1.25/10.00, GPT-4o 2.50/10.00, Claude Sonnet 4.6 3.00/15.00, Claude Opus 4.6 15.00/75.00. The cheap tier clusters at 0.10 USD/1M input.
fx: Frontier-tier input pricing (e.g. Claude Opus / GPT-5 class) drops below 1.00 USD/1M input, collapsing the 100x cheap-to-frontier spread.

s: Heavy-tier 50M tok/day local 36-mo TCO $391,707 = $7.15/M vs OpenAI $6.90/M, Anthropic $9.86/M, open-weight APIs $1.97/M
stmt: In a 2026 TCO model at heavy sustained volume (50M tokens/day), owned-hardware local inference reaches ~{"cur":"USD","val":7.15}/1M tokens over 36 months (Year-1 $308,347; Years 2-3 ~$41,680/yr electricity+labor once depreciated), roughly at parity with OpenAI cloud (~6.90 USD/1M) but above Anthropic (~9.86) and well above open-weight APIs (~1.97). Below ~10M tokens/day fixed hardware cost dominates and cloud per-use wins. EU power ($0.25-0.30/kWh) pushes the local break-even 40-60% higher in required daily volume; 2026 break-even points sit ~40% lower than 2024.
fx: A measured deployment shows local heavy-tier effective cost diverging >50% from the $7.15/M model (e.g. due to real utilization, refresh at 24-30mo, or GPU street prices 20-50% over MSRP).

s: LLM inference cost ~10x/yr decline, ~1000x in 3yr for constant capability
stmt: For a constant capability level, LLM inference cost has fallen on the order of 10x per year (~1000x over three years). This is a secondary/community-sourced trend, not a measured rate card, but it is directionally consistent with the GPT-4o input drop from 2.50 to lower-tier successors.
fx: Per-capability inference price declines stall below 2x/yr for two consecutive years.

s: Crossover favours local at high sustained volume, cloud at low/bursty
mech: Canvas OS ml layer runs a cost/latency/privacy-aware router: on-device (Ollama/Core ML) for light, bursty, or privacy-sensitive calls; cheap cloud APIs (Nano/Flash/Mistral Small at ~$0.10/1M) for high-volume simple tasks; frontier cloud only for hard reasoning. This is the cost realisation of the existing wm-arch-google-intent-router directive.

s: Egress is the dominant hidden cost; keep data near compute
mech: Canvas OS sovereignty/tape layer keeps the append-only tape and user data local-first (on-device or single-region), so inference reads data in place and egress tax is never paid. Doubles as the compliance posture for the regulation layer (GDPR Art.44 transfer minimisation).

rec: Build the inference router as a first-class ml-layer component with a pluggable cost model (per-token cloud rates + amortised on-device $/token) so routing decisions track the live crossover rather than a hardcoded vendor.
fx: On-device inference for our actual workload never beats the cheapest cloud API on total cost, making local routing dead weight.

s: CRN Q4 2025 reports big three ~68% combined; figures differ from Synergy Q3 63%
q: Which is the current authoritative share figure - Synergy Q3 2025 (63% combined; 29/20/13) or the CRN-reported Q4 2025 (~68% combined; ~28/21/15 per TechTarget)? The metrics may differ (CIS vs total enterprise cloud spend).
w: The world-map should anchor on one consistent quarter+definition or it will misstate the degree of compute concentration our wedge must route around.

q: What is on-device inference cost for OUR actual Canvas OS workload (token mix, context lengths, latency target) on a baseline user device - not the sitepoint heavy-tier datacenter model?
w: The crossover claim is modeled on 50M tok/day datacenter hardware; a consumer-device router needs the consumer-device curve (Apple Neural Engine / M-series unified memory throughput), which is not yet grounded.

q: What are the economics and access terms of Apple Private Cloud Compute and Apple/Google on-device foundation-model APIs for third-party OS-layer apps?
w: If Apple/Google expose subsidised on-device or PCC inference to first-party apps but not to us, the hybrid-router cost model shifts and a moat opens against our wedge.
